principle: Evaluative adjectives signal intent to convince but don't convince — reader needs to experience it themselves
explanation: X.copy|eval_adjectives: "incredible/exclusive/premium/exquisite" -> reader_recognizes_manipulation NOT feels_quality; evaluation == authors_conclusion_forced_on_reader; NO:stacking_adjectives_for_impact; replace_with_experience eg: "incredible_quality_low_price" >> facts + scenarios + demonstration

principle: Each evaluation hides a story the author was too lazy to tell — the adjective is a shortcut past real work
explanation: X.copy|eval_as_shortcut: adjective == compressed_story_author_skipped; "eco-friendly" hides sourcing + manufacturing + certification; author_thinks_one_word == job_done; NO:false_sense_of_completed_work; unpack_each_claim_into_its_story eg: "eco-friendly_floors" >> "wood_from_sustainable_forests_+_natural_resin_coatings_safe_for_nurseries"

principle: Back evaluations with contractable facts — but empty facts unknown to reader are useless
explanation: X.copy|facts_for_eval: fact == something_you_can_put_in_contract; reader_draws_own_conclusion_from_facts -> stronger_than_your_evaluation; BUT unknown_fact == empty; NO:citing_obscure_ratings/acronyms eg: "quality_content" >> "articles_by_Business_School_professors"; "member_of_RSVA_ICC" -> means_nothing_to_reader

principle: Vanity facts serve the company not the reader — replace with reader scenarios
explanation: X.copy|vanity_facts: "180_classrooms_in_40_cities_90_programs" -> impresses_company NOT reader; reader_needs_one_classroom_near_them; NO:facts_of_grandeur; translate_scale_into_reader_benefit eg: "10_years_180_classrooms_40_cities" >> "classrooms_in_business_centers_so_you_come_after_work"

principle: Stories create mental movies — reader draws own conclusions and generalizes beyond your examples
explanation: X.copy|stories_over_eval: scenario -> reader_imagines_situation -> arrives_at_conclusion_independently -> holds_it_stronger; stories_expand: describe_one_case -> reader_infers_many_similar; NO:abstract_praise eg: "we_handle_complex_data_recovery" >> "phone_fell_from_window/pulled_from_sea/run_over_by_car_—_bring_it"

principle: Demonstrate sensory qualities instead of describing them — text handles specs, visuals handle emotions
explanation: X.copy|demonstration: feelings_cant_be_written_convincingly; "sharp_knife" -> show_chef_cutting; "creative_freedom" -> show_person_creating; text_role: specs + facts; visual_role: emotions + identity; NO:writing_feelings_as_adjectives eg: "premium_backpacks_for_adventurous_spirits" >> "backpacks_for_forest_steppe_desert" + photos_of_confident_people

principle: In business emails replace importance-evaluations with why and what exactly — praise with impact not adjectives
explanation: X.copy|business_eval: "very_important/critical/urgent" -> empty_pressure; explain_WHY + WHAT_specifically; praise: adjectives_only_work_from_authority; without_authority -> describe_impact_instead; NO:evaluative_manipulation eg: "very_important_task_react_fast" >> "call_center_=_60%_revenue_react_within_30min"

principle: Amplifiers weaken what follows — delete without loss; blind-name test reveals empty text
explanation: X.copy|amplifiers: "absolutely/extremely/truly/incredibly" -> stacked_on_evaluation == desperation_not_conviction; delete_amplifier -> meaning_intact; blind_test: cover_company_name -> if_text_fits_any_company == empty; NO:amplifying_emptiness eg: "absolutely_free" >> "free"; "maximally_quality_repair" >> "repair_with_guarantee"
